highlights.title: Consolidate Omnipod options into OmnipodKit
changes: Pump picker now shows a single "All Omnipod Types" option instead of separate Eros and DASH entries.
Onboarding and pump setup use one Omnipod choice instead of separate Eros/DASH options.
Omnipod onboarding basal-rate picker allows 0 U/hr (DASH minimum), which may not enforce Eros's 0.05 U/hr minimum.
Privacy and telemetry texts now reference OmnipodKit instead of the removed OmniKit/OmniBLE.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1200: Remove Legacy Omnipod Submodules (OmniKit, OmniBLE)

includedPullRequests.title: Remove Legacy Omnipod Submodules (OmniKit, OmniBLE)
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
Removes the legacy `OmniBLE` (DASH) and `OmniKit` (Eros) submodules and all their platform-specific code paths, consolidating both Omnipod variants under the new `OmnipodKit` submodule (`OmniPumpManager`), added and shipped with Trio `v0.8.1`.

**Changes:**
- Deletes `OmniBLE` and `OmniKit` git submodules and their `.gitmodules` entries
- Bumps `OmnipodKit` to `d68699c`
- Removes `OmniBLEPumpManager` and `OmnipodPumpManager` from `staticPumpManagers`, `DeviceDataManager`, `PumpSetupView`, and all pump-type enums — replaced by `OmniPumpManager`
- Collapses the separate "Omnipod Eros" / "Omnipod DASH" pump-selection buttons into a single "All Omnipod Types" entry in `HomeRootView` and `PumpConfigRootView`
- Merges `omnipodEros` / `omnipodDash` onboarding enum cases into a single `.omni` case; adds a `FIXME` noting that Eros min-basal-rate differentiation is not yet handled
- Removes OmniBLE/OmniKit build targets, framework references, and test schemes from `project.pbxproj` and `Trio.xcscheme`
- Drops OmniBLE/OmniKit Crowdin localisation sources and swiftformat exclusions
- Updates telemetry comment and `PRIVACY_POLICY.md` to reference OmnipodKit instead of OmniBLE

**Note:** The `.omni` onboarding basal-rate picker currently uses the DASH minimum (0 U/hr). Eros requires a minimum of 0.05 U/hr but the two variants can no longer be distinguished at that step — tracked in the inline `FIXME`.
